sourceMovie.title: Belladonna of Sadness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Belladonna of Sadness works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Waking Life
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Belladonna of Sadness clicked for you, Waking Life (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mind Game
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Belladonna of Sadness clicked for you, Mind Game (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Belladonna of Sadness?
faq.answer: Try Waking Life, Mind Game, Kizumonogatari Part 1: Tekketsu—each shares DNA with Belladonna of Sadness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Belladonna of Sadness?
faq.answer: Belladonna of Sadness (1973) is best described as Animation, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Belladonna of Sadness worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
